---
title: "report"
output: html_document
---

[introduction]

```{r}
par(mfrow=c(1,3))
x <- runif(50)
hist(x, main='Histogram of 50 draws')
y <- runif(500)
hist(y, main='Histogram of 500 draws')
z <- runif(5000)
hist(z, main='Histogram of 5000 draws')
```

[discussion of efficacy of random number generator]


[introduction to meat of assignment]

[B: simulating random variable S with uniformly distributed variable U]
```{r}
n<-10

sim.coin <- function(n) {
  U<-runif(n, -1, 1)
  print(U)
  for (i in 1:n) {
    if (U[i]<0) {
      U[i]<--1
    }
    else {
      U[i]<-1
    }
  }
  return (U)
}

sim.coin(n)
```
[C 3650 cumsum(sim.coin(n))]

```{r}
n<-3650

sum<-1:3650
positive<-0
cumsum <- function(x) {
  sum[1]<- x[1]
  for (i in 2:3650) {
    sum[i]<- sum[i-1] + x[i-1]
    if (sum[i]>0) {
      positive<-positive + 1

      }
  }
  positive<-positive/3650
  print("Fraction of time the balance is positive: ")
  print(positive)
  return (sum)
}

balance<-cumsum(sim.coin(n))
plot(1:3650,balance,type='l',xlab="Number of Trials", ylab="Balance")
```

[we expect that the balance is positive half the time, is this the case in your generated realization?]

No, because in 

[E: a graph where the balance is positive only 5 percent of the time seems highly unlikely. Make a guess at the probability of this happening]


[F:]
